scenario_title: Add CNAME at a name that already has other data
test_rationale: This test sets up a legal owner name that already contains ordinary data, then issues a single UPDATE that tries to add a CNAME at that same name. That makes the update syntactically valid but semantically conflicting under RFC 2181. The subsequent CNAME and A queries provide a stable oracle for whether the server preserved the original RRset set and whether the CNAME was installed, while the UPDATE reply itself captures the underspecified error mapping.

group_1.behavior: Rejects the conflicting CNAME addition and preserves the existing A/NS data.
group_1.post_state: host.campus.edu. still has A (and NS) data; no CNAME RRset is present.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the conflicting CNAME addition with a different update failure mapping or conflict handling outcome.
group_2.post_state: host.campus.edu. still has A (and NS) data; no CNAME RRset is present.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 says a CNAME cannot coexist with other data, but RFC 2136 does not specify which UPDATE error code to use for an add_rr that attempts to create that conflict, nor whether the server must treat it as an atomic no-op versus another rejection path. As a result, BIND and Knot can diverge in the UPDATE reply RCODE while leaving the post-state unchanged.
